differentials.name: adducted lower extremity flexed at hip
differentials.raw: adducted lower extremity flexed at hip

differentials.name: lower extremity in external rotation [CENTRAL ACETABULAR FRACTURE-DISLOCATION]
Surgery: fixation of osteochondral fragments if > 1 cm2, medial capsule repair, lateral retinacular release, vastus medialis et lateralis rearrangement, medial retinaculum reefing
differentials.raw: Surgery: fixation of osteochondral fragments if > 1 cm2, medial capsule repair, lateral retinacular release, vastus medialis et lateralis rearrangement, medial retinaculum reefing

POSTERIOR HIP DISLOCATION: Frequency:   5% of all dislocations Mechanism:   classical dashboard injury (= flexed knee strikes dashboard) Associated with:   fractures of posterior rim of acetabulum, femoral head Mechanism:   forced abduction + external rotation Associated with:   fractures of acetabular rim, greater trochanter, femoral neck, femoral head (characteristic depression on posterosuperior and lateral portion) Subtypes:   1.   anterior obturator dislocation
obturator position of femoral head Mechanism:   force applied to lateral side of trochanter Patellar Dislocation =  TRANSI

context: Frequency:   5% of all dislocations Mechanism:   classical dashboard injury (= flexed knee strikes dashboard) Associated with:   fractures of posterior rim of acetabulum, femoral head Mechanism:   forced abduction + external rotation Associated with:   fractures of acetabular rim, greater trochanter, femoral neck, femoral head (characteristic depression on posterosuperior and lateral portion) Subtypes:   1.   anterior obturator dislocation √  prominent lesser trochanter √  obturator position of femoral head Mechanism:   force applied to lateral side of trochanter Patellar Dislocation =  TRANSI
